steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: An opening parenthesis after “stockade” marks a parenthetical phrase defining what a stockade is. This structure parallels (a halter) for “hackamore”.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
The structure is: "stockade" (a ______ followed by "comes from estacada." An opening parenthesis appears after "stockade" — it must be closed before the sentence continues. The rule: parentheses must be paired; a closing parenthesis is the only mark that can close an opening one.
  • Choice A — fence—: "stockade" (a fence— comes from estacada" — a dash cannot close an opening parenthesis; the parenthetical would remain unclosed. ✗
  • Choice B — fence,: "stockade" (a fence, comes from estacada" — a comma cannot close an opening parenthesis; this leaves the parenthetical open and inserts an invalid comma. ✗
  • Choice C — fence;: "stockade" (a fence; comes from estacada" — a semicolon cannot close an opening parenthesis, and what follows is not an independent clause anyway. ✗
  • Choice D — fence): "stockade" (a fence) comes from estacada" — the closing parenthesis pairs with the opening one, completing the parenthetical definition cleanly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
